response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals, mentoring ability, and architectural thinking, this role requires deep C++ expertise and industrial automation domain knowledge. The candidate's primary C++ experience (6 months at National Instruments, 10 years ago) is significantly outdated, and the last decade has focused on TypeScript/Node.js web development—a different technical domain entirely. Additionally, there is no evidence of industrial protocols, IoT systems, message brokers, MQTT, or IEC 62443 security standards experience, which are core to the role. This would require 6-12 months of ramp-up, unsuitable for a mid-senior position requiring immediate technical contribution.
strengths: Proven software craftsmanship with TDD, CI/CD, and clean architecture across multiple systems
Experience building low-latency, high-performance distributed systems handling millions of requests
Strong mentoring and communication demonstrated through workshops, teaching roles, and open-source contributions
concerns: C++ skills are severely outdated (only 6 months of dedicated experience, 10+ years ago); recent 10-year career in TypeScript/Node.js represents a significant technical drift
Zero experience with industrial automation, IoT platforms, or specialized industrial protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, IEC 104, OPC UA)
No demonstrated knowledge of Secure Development Lifecycle, IEC 62443 standards, or embedded systems security—core requirements for the role
